claim: Memory bandwidth, not raw compute, is the primary bottleneck for transformer inference workloads
actionable: When optimizing inference systems for transformers, prioritize memory bandwidth efficiency over raw FLOP count; matrix-vector operations are memory-bound with 1:1 FLOP-to-byte ratio
source_title: Accelerators @ 3x NVIDIA H200 perf, Made in the USA - Thomas Sohmers + Mitesh Agrawal, Positron AI

claim: Matrix-vector multiplication in transformer inference is essentially uncacheable due to massive model weight sizes (tens of GB to terabytes)
actionable: Design inference hardware assuming weights cannot fit in cache; focus on memory architecture that enables streaming of weight data
source_title: Accelerators @ 3x NVIDIA H200 perf, Made in the USA - Thomas Sohmers + Mitesh Agrawal, Positron AI

claim: NVIDIA H100 achieves only ~29% of theoretical memory bandwidth on Llama 70B; modern GPU generations are achieving decreasing percentages of theoretical bandwidth
actionable: When benchmarking GPU inference, measure actual achieved memory bandwidth percentage, not theoretical specs; Hopper to Blackwell shows declining efficiency despite doubled theoretical bandwidth
source_title: Accelerators @ 3x NVIDIA H200 perf, Made in the USA - Thomas Sohmers + Mitesh Agrawal, Positron AI

claim: FPGA-based inference accelerators can achieve 93% memory bandwidth utilization for transformer workloads while GPUs saturate much lower
actionable: Consider FPGA implementations for transformer-specific inference even though FPGAs are traditionally thought to be power-inefficient; the right architecture can overcome FPGA inefficiencies
source_title: Accelerators @ 3x NVIDIA H200 perf, Made in the USA - Thomas Sohmers + Mitesh Agrawal, Positron AI

claim: Shipping production silicon within 15 months of company founding is extremely fast; most historical accelerator companies take 3-5 years between product iterations
actionable: Narrow product focus and avoiding custom compilers/framework rewrites enables rapid time-to-market for semiconductor startups
source_title: Accelerators @ 3x NVIDIA H200 perf, Made in the USA - Thomas Sohmers + Mitesh Agrawal, Positron AI

claim: Supporting raw CUDA ecosystem binary weights as input (no recompilation needed) is critical for accelerator adoption; AMD's separate PyTorch distribution being 6-9 months behind caused adoption failures
actionable: Design inference accelerators to accept pre-trained CUDA weights directly; zero recompilation steps are mandatory for user adoption—even one compilation step is too many
source_title: Accelerators @ 3x NVIDIA H200 perf, Made in the USA - Thomas Sohmers + Mitesh Agrawal, Positron AI

claim: Hardcoding transformer-specific architectures into silicon is risky; rapid architectural innovations (like DeepSeek's attention changes) can make specialized chips obsolete within months
actionable: Build general-purpose linear algebra accelerators rather than transformer-specific chips; design for the underlying mathematical primitives that remain stable
source_title: Accelerators @ 3x NVIDIA H200 perf, Made in the USA - Thomas Sohmers + Mitesh Agrawal, Positron AI

claim: Token generation (decode) phase dominates latency for reasoning models; input-to-output ratios have flipped from 10:1 (prefill-heavy) to 100:1 (generation-heavy) with reasoning
actionable: Optimize inference systems for decode performance; reasoning models now generate significantly more tokens than they receive as input, making generation speed the critical metric
source_title: Accelerators @ 3x NVIDIA H200 perf, Made in the USA - Thomas Sohmers + Mitesh Agrawal, Positron AI

claim: Large KV cache storage enables massive performance improvements by avoiding recomputation of system prompts and context on each request
actionable: Design inference hardware with large on-chip memory to cache KV values; this reduces true prefill computation needed when serving repeated context
source_title: Accelerators @ 3x NVIDIA H200 perf, Made in the USA - Thomas Sohmers + Mitesh Agrawal, Positron AI

claim: Selling hardware directly to customers (not running cloud services) allows accelerator makers to prove ROI to customers and accelerates adoption
actionable: For accelerator startups, focus on hardware sales directly to cloud providers and enterprises rather than building proprietary cloud services; ROIC is the key metric customers use
source_title: Accelerators @ 3x NVIDIA H200 perf, Made in the USA - Thomas Sohmers + Mitesh Agrawal, Positron AI

claim: Small teams (27 people) enable rapid silicon development by forcing focus on specific applications and avoiding feature bloat
actionable: Maintain small, focused teams for accelerator development; avoid expanding scope until core product delivers clear value
source_title: Accelerators @ 3x NVIDIA H200 perf, Made in the USA - Thomas Sohmers + Mitesh Agrawal, Positron AI
